endpoints.name: GET /v1/{name}
endpoints.description: Looks up a single pipeline. Returns a "NOT_FOUND" error if no such pipeline exists. Returns a "FORBIDDEN" error if the caller doesn't have permission to access it.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{NAME}} with a real name.

endpoints.name: DELETE /v1/{name}
endpoints.description: Deletes a pipeline. If a scheduler job is attached to the pipeline, it will be deleted.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{NAME}} with a real name.

endpoints.name: PATCH /v1/{name}
endpoints.description: Updates a pipeline. If successful, the updated Pipeline is returned. Returns `NOT_FOUND` if the pipeline doesn't exist. If UpdatePipeline does not return successfully, you can retry the UpdatePipeline request until you receive a successful response.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{NAME}} with a real name.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{name}:run
endpoints.description: Creates a job for the specified pipeline directly. You can use this method when the internal scheduler is not configured and you want to trigger the job directly or through an external system. Returns a "NOT_FOUND" error if the pipeline doesn't exist. Returns a "FORBIDDEN" error if the user doesn't have permission to access the pipeline or run jobs for the pipeline.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{NAME}} with a real name.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{name}:stop
endpoints.description: Freezes pipeline execution permanently. If there's a corresponding scheduler entry, it's deleted, and the pipeline state is changed to "ARCHIVED". However, pipeline metadata is retained.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{NAME}} with a real name.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{parent}/jobs
endpoints.description: Lists jobs for a given pipeline. Throws a "FORBIDDEN" error if the caller doesn't have permission to access it.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{parent}/pipelines
endpoints.description: Lists pipelines. Returns a "FORBIDDEN" error if the caller doesn't have permission to access it.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}/pipelines
endpoints.description: Creates a pipeline. For a batch pipeline, you can pass scheduler information. Data Pipelines uses the scheduler information to create an internal scheduler that runs jobs periodically. If the internal scheduler is not configured, you can use RunPipeline to run jobs.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.
